Caption: Characteristic flame shape of the CH 4 H 2 –air mixtures (a) Instantaneous (left half) and time-averaged (right half) heat release rate.

Stream-wise heat release rate distribution, q y ¯ , and normalized OH* chemiluminescence for the CH 4 H 2 –air mixtures. Characteristic flame length, H f , from LES (dotted line). The origin is located at the dump plane.

Instantaneous scatter data for temperature, T , versus equivalence ratio, ϕ , colored by heat release rate, Q ̇ . The scatter data include the points with Q ̇ > Q ̇ min = 0 . 1 Q ̇ max ( X H 2 = 0 ) . Solid lines show the adiabatic flame temperature (upper solid line) and the temperature at Q ̇ max in a 1D free-flame (lower solid line). Global equivalence ratio ϕ = 0 . 8 .

Instantaneous (left half) and time-averaged (right half) equivalence ratio distribution for X H 2 equal to 0.0 and 0.6. Iso-contour of instantaneous and time-averaged heat release rates at 0.2 Q ̇ max (solid black line).

(a) Time-averaged equivalence ratio distribution of the fuel injection in one swirler channel and (b) trajectory of the jet-in-crossflow according to scaling law with A = 3/0.73 and B = 1/3 [88]. Height of the swirler channel top wall (dotted line).

Overall flame frequency response F ( ω ) . Confidence interval from SysID ± σ (shaded area). Results from sinusoidal mono-frequency forcing ( × ). Results from MISO SysID reconstructed according to Eq. (10) for X H 2 = 0.2 and 0.6 (dashed line).
Flame frequency response to velocity fluctuations F u ( ω ) . Confidence interval from SysID ± σ (shaded area). Frequencies for ψ u − θ equal to an odd multiple of π (dotted line).
Flame frequency response to equivalence ratio fluctuations F ϕ ( ω ) . Confidence interval from SysID ± σ (shaded area).
Equivalence ratio response to air flow rate fluctuations H ϕ ( ω ) . Results from sinusoidal mono-frequency forcing ( × ).
Sketch of the MISO model structure for the flame and mixing transfer functions in a partially premixed flame with a stiff injector and compact mixer ( u r ′ = u ′ ).

Flame frequency response to the equivalence ratio fluctuations produced by velocity fluctuations, H ϕ ( ω ) F ϕ ( ω ) , and phase difference between the flame response to velocity and equivalence ratio fluctuations, ψ ϕ − u . Interval from − π / 2 to π / 2 (shaded area).
Response of volumetric heat of reaction ( ρ h ̃ R ), flame speed ( S ̃ L ), and flame area ( A f ) to equivalence ratio fluctuations.
Flame frequency response to equivalence ratio fluctuations reconstructed from F ϕ = F h R + F S L + F A .
